data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning Accenture Stock In The Last 20 Years
data.description:
Accenture (NYSE:ACN) has outperformed the market over the past 20 years by 2.84% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 11.64%. Currently, Accenture has a market capitalization of $175.29 billion.

Buying $1000 In ACN: If an investor had bought $1000 of ACN stock 20 years ago, it would be worth $9,070.63 today based on a price of $284.83 for ACN at the time of writing.

Accenture's Performance Over Last 20 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.

This article was generated by Benzinga's automated content engine and reviewed by an editor.

data.title: This Starbucks Analyst Turns Bullish; Here Are Top 5 Upgrades For Friday
data.description:

data.title: Plug Powers Europe's Energy Transition With 100 MW Hydrogen Build
data.description:
Plug Power, Inc. (NASDAQ:PLUG) on Friday disclosed the completion of 100 MW of PEM GenEco electrolyzer installations at Galp’s Sines Refinery.

This marks a key milestone in one of Europe’s largest renewable hydrogen projects.

Details

The hydrogen and fuel cell company says that since the project’s launch in October 2025 with the first 10 MW GenEco module, all ten arrays totaling 100 MW have now been delivered and installed at the refinery.

Commissioning is set to begin soon, and once operational, the system is expected to generate up to 15,000 tons of renewable hydrogen annually, offsetting about 20% of the refinery’s grey hydrogen use and cutting greenhouse gas emissions by 110,000 tons CO₂e per year (Scope 1 and 2).

Notably, Plug’s GenEco electrolyzers leverage a global supply chain, combining U.S. and European-manufactured components with scalable PEM technology.

The Sines initiative is part of Plug’s broader European expansion, including multi-gigawatt deployments across Spain, the U.K., and beyond, supported by a $2 billion global pipeline.

Management Commentary

Benjamin Haycraft, Chief Strategy Officer at Plug, said, “We continue to invest across the region to support large-scale decarbonization, and this milestone demonstrates our ability to deliver real infrastructure that advances Europe’s energy transition and strengthens long-term energy resilience.”

Walmart Deal

This month, in a recent SEC filing, the company said it entered into an agreement with retailer Walmart Inc. (NASDAQ:WMT) to give it a contingent, limited-use license to access and use certain escrowed GenKey System-related materials.

PLUG Price Action: Plug Power shares were up 3.47% at $2.68 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: Diginex Targets Brazil's $77 Billion Decarbonization Boom, Stock Soars
data.description:
Diginex Limited (NASDAQ:DGNX) stock rose Friday following the company’s announcement of a Joint Venture Framework Agreement to support sustainability and decarbonization initiatives in Brazil.

This news comes as broader markets declined, with the Russell 2000 down 0.37% and the S&P 500 down 0.19%, suggesting DGNX’s rise may be driven by company-specific developments rather than broader market trends.

DGNX Announces Brazil ESG Joint Venture

Diginex signed a Joint Venture Framework Agreement with BGlobal (Brazil, Brand & Business) and the State of Mato Grosso to develop a digital infrastructure platform for large-scale decarbonization and ESG reporting, initially focused on the beef sector with potential expansion to other industries.

The platform is intended to deliver standardized, auditable sustainability and emissions data aligned with international standards, support a “Digital Green Passport” to enhance export market access, and generate MRV-ready data for credible carbon and nature-based initiatives.

The collaboration brings together Diginex and its subsidiary PlanA. earth’s ESG reporting and emissions accounting capabilities with BGlobal’s local stakeholder engagement, while complementing Mato Grosso’s existing “Passaporte Verde” program.

Covering more than 900,000 km and accounting for approximately 60% of its territory, Mato Grosso is a globally significant agricultural region. Brazil’s decarbonization market is projected to grow from approximately $43.1 billion in 2024 to $76.8 billion by 2030, reinforcing the initiative’s strategic relevance.

DGNX Stock Faces Bearish Technicals

The stock is currently trading 57.7% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 86.2% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a bearish trend in the short- to medium-term. Shares have decreased by 15.30% over the past 12 months and are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting ongoing challenges for the company.

The RSI is at 18.85, indicating the stock is oversold, while the MACD is above its signal line, suggesting a potential bullish reversal. The combination of oversold RSI and bullish MACD suggests bullish momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $3.50
  • Key Support: $0.50

Upcoming DGNX Earnings Report Anticipated

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on July 11, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: $0.07

DGNX Price Action: Diginex shares were up 32.59% at $1.79 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by T. Schneider via Shutterstock

data.title: Upper-Middle-Class Americans Share The Smartest Money Moves They Made Last Year.  'The Material Purchases Provide Us Less And Less Joy'
data.description:
Reddit’s r/UpperMiddleFinance has become a surprisingly practical look at how people are managing their money in a high-cost, high-volatility world. From paying off mortgages and skipping luxury upgrades to doubling down on long-term investments, upper-middle-class Americans say these were their best financial decisions in the last year.

Prioritizing Intentional Spending Over Flash

One of the most recurring themes was a shift away from materialism and toward intentional living. One person summed it up plainly: “The material purchases provide us less and less joy as we have gotten older… We receive way more fulfillment from spending on travel and experiences.”

Don't Miss:

  • The AI Marketing Platform Backed by Insiders from Google, Meta, and Amazon — Invest at $0.85/Share
  • Warren Buffett once said, "If you don't find a way to make money while you sleep, you will work until you die." Here’s how you can earn passive income with just $10, starting today.

The original poster kicked things off by sharing that their family of four passed on home renovations to instead spend three weeks in Italy. “We all decided to continue prioritizing experiences over material stuff,” they wrote. “Also shows the kids how we balance, budget, and prioritize our hard-earned dollars.”

Another user echoed that approach, saying they paid cash for a used Cadillac instead of splurging on something new. “It's so tempting to splurge, but all cars get old eventually.”

Debt Payoff Was A Big Theme

Dozens of commenters shared how they focused on eliminating debt. One household paid off their mortgage 10 years early. Another person dropped $35,000 to pay off a loan and ended up 42% up on the year. One person shared, “We still maxed out our 401(k)s but any bonuses and extra savings went to the loan.”

One popular strategy: using 0% interest balance transfer cards to pay down credit card debt. A federal worker wrote that combining that tactic with a side gig helped them feel like they were finally back on track after a rough stretch.

Trending: Americans With a Financial Plan Can 4X Their Wealth — Get Your Personalized Plan from a CFP Pro

Investing Through The Noise

Others said their smartest move was simply staying calm. “To quote [Vanguard Group founder] Jack Bogle, ‘Don't just do something! Stand there!'” one person wrote. Another said they maxed their 401(k) during a market dip, then dollar-cost averaged the rest of the year.

Some got more aggressive. One poster said they invested over $150,000 total in the last year, including $77,000 into tax-advantaged accounts. Others shifted contributions to backdoor Roth IRAs or took advantage of mega backdoor opportunities from their employers.

One commenter noted they reallocated car savings into silver instead of upgrading their vehicle, and it paid off. Another held their Nvidia stock and opened a new gold position.

Adding Options Beyond Stocks

With many upper-middle-class Americans maxing out traditional investment accounts, some are looking to diversify beyond public markets. Platforms like Fundrise are growing in popularity for exactly that reason, offering access to private market opportunities with just a $10 minimum. For many investors who want to stay consistent while branching out, it's becoming a compelling option.

Whether it was skipping stuff they didn't need, sticking with their investments through a rough patch, or finally paying off old debts, people were focused on being thoughtful with their money. The most-liked comments weren't about hitting it big, but about making steady progress, feeling more in control, and choosing what really mattered to them.

Image: Shutterstock
